Updated: 1/21/2001
Important news in blue



FUNimation has picked up two more anime: Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender



Season 5 is currently in production. Air date is currently unknown.



New DBZ on YTV (The episodes air when YTV receives them. The schedule is very fluid).



Viz will probably release future graphic novels uncensored. A press release is expected soon.



Movie #4, #5, and #6 slated for U.S. release in 2001.



Infogrames will be the publisher of the upcoming DBZ games. More information can be found in our Infogrames feature.


DVD (FUNimation):

Bardock:
The Father of Goku
(Released)

Mystical Adventure
(2/27/2001)

Android DVDs
(Spring 2001)


VHS (FUNimation):

Imperfect Cell:
17's End
(2/20/2001)

Perfect Cell:
Hunt for 18
(2/20/2001)
Temptation
(4/17/2001)
Perfection
(4/17/2001)
Unstoppable
(6/19/2001)

Cell Games:
Ultimatum
(6/19/2001)

Great Saiya Man:
Declaration
(3/20/2001)
Gohan's Secret
(3/20/2001)
Crash Course
(5/15/2001)

World Tournament:
Junior Division
(5/15/2001)


Manga (Viz):

DB Part 3:
#9
(2/9/2001)
#10
(3/7/2001)
#11
(4/20/2001)
#12
(5/4/2001)

DBZ Part 4:
#3
(2/9/2001)
#4
(3/14/2001)
#5
(4/13/2001)
#6
(5/11/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:
Vol. 4
(2/9/2001)
Vol. 5
(5/18/2001)

DBZ Graphic Novels:
Vol. 4
(3/7/2001)




  B.A.D. Mini-Shrine
  Ginga GIRI GIRI
  Kienzan's Edge

*We are NOT accepting hosting submissions*

Latest Feature: Steve Simmons Interview
Thursday - 02/01/2001

Mr.E @ 22:09 pst - May Viz Releases...
Very interesting...

Viz will be releasing Animerica Extra Vol. 4 5, Aqua Knight, Part 3 2,Art of Pokemon: Third Movie, Dragonball, Part 3 11, Dragonball Z, Part 4 5, Eagle Vol. 15: Nomination, El Hazard, Part 2 3, Gundam Wing: Blind Target 3, Gundam Wing: Episode Zero 1, Gundam Wing Vol. 1: Ground Zero (tpb), Genesis Evangelion Book 5 (collector's ed.) 7 , No Need for Tenchi!, Part 11 1, Pulp Vol. 5 5, Ranma 1/2, Part 10 1, Silent Mobius Vol. 6: Catastrophe (tpb), Yellow Caballero Pokemon 2 and Video Girl Ai Vol. 3: Recall (tpb). They will also be re-releasing, Dragonball Z Vol. 2 (tpb), Mobile Suit Gundam: 0079 Vol. 1 (tpb), Mobile Suit Gundam: 0079 Vol. 2 (tpb) , Ranma 1/2 Vol. 16 (tpb) and Strain Vol. 4 (tpb).

Note the text in bold. Re-releasing? Does this mean the new release will be uncut? Our petition was not unheard, I assure you. It's very possible that this will be an unedited graphic novel. It's also possible that they are just refershing retail inventories. We'll see in the near future...I'd expect an announcement in March. Via UMJAMS.

Mr.E @ 13:16 pst - Australia Convention...
Some convention news for those in Survivor country:

Dragon Ball Z is the latest craze among kids and adults in Australia. It is fast becoming favorite cartoon series for those living Down Under. Actors Chris Sabat (Vegeta, & Piccolo) and Sean Schemmel (Goku) have taken a trip to Aussie land. They will be signing autographs and acting out scenes from the series at Comic-Fest 2001. Some are calling the actor's appearance at Comic-Fest the biggest attraction at the convention. Stay tuned we will let you know details from the trip soon.

It's safe to say the VAs are taking a small break from voicing for now...gives FUNi a chance to catch up with the DVDs. Via the official site.

Mr.E @ 12:02 pst - Desktops Update...
Been a while since I've updated this fan-submission section...but here's your latest dose of desktop madness. Aye, there were a lot of bad submissions. Desktops aren't supposed to be collages, people (At least the ones I accept). Using your own fanart is a plus. Using scans in an imaginative way is a plus. Slapping a couple of Vegita screenshots together and sending it in is a big minus. MonkeyJay demostrates what I like in a desktop submission (image of the moment).
Wednesday - 01/31/2001

Kaio @ 10:24 pst - Strength in Numbers
I've made a bunch of small updates, mostly just to clean out my mailbox. First, the DVD subsection of the merchandise section has been updated to contain the new subtitled DVDs. Second, two new pages have been added to Walk this World, in the fanmanga section, and a new chapter has been added to Crono's Story in the fanfic section. Additionally, a new card strategy has been added to the card strategies section. Well, I think that's it. Just a bunch of small udpates.
Tuesday - 01/30/2001

Mr.E @ 16:52 pst - Gen Comments on Two New Anime...
Some more info regarding Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender. Here's the original e-mail sent to Gen:

1. For Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender, will you be using voice actors from Dragon Ball Z?

2. Will you be getting any of the Movies/Specials of any of the 2 series?

3. Will the original dialog be kept or will it be changed like Dragon Ball Z's?

Here's Gen's response:

1. Only if we feel it is a very good match.

2. A 90 minute Blue Gender movie is currently under production in Japan and we have the rights for it. For YYH, we do not have those rights for the movies/specials since they were granted many years ago to another company.

3. Blue Gender's dialog will be more of a direct translation. YYH will need to be adjusted for mass market broadcast on a network.

Gen

Many thanks to SSJ4Kain who submitted the above exchange. Blah, "adjusted for mass market." America can suck it, quite honestly. All FUNimation needs to watch out for is explicatives...I don't want lame humor thrown in there because they think fans want it. At least Blue Gender will be pretty much untouched.

Kaio @ 16:23 pst - VHS Merchandise Updated!
Folks, this update was long, long overdue. The VHS section of the merchandise page has finally been updated. It was an incredibly boring job, which is why I put it off for so long, but it really needed to be done. Next up is the DVDs section, which should take a lot less time because it's a lot smaller (unfortunately).

Mr.E @ 14:42 pst - CCG Luv...
For all you card players out there:

The Trunks Saga release date has been rescheduled. The release, now scheduled for March, has been delayed for several reasons including major design and production changes, which translate into a more exciting game! The Trunks Saga release will feature the debut of the Dragon Ball Z CCG new card designs. DBZ CCG insiders can't say enough good things about the look and style of the new cards. "These cards are going to go a long way to making the game easier and even more fun to play," said Jim Ward, DBZ CCG Game Designer. The Trunks Saga release will feature the debut of the Dragon Ball Z CCG new card designs. DBZ CCG insiders can't say enough good things about the look and style of the new cards. "These cards are going to go a long way to making the game easier and even more fun to play," said Jim Ward, DBZ CCG Game Designer. The new card design of the Trunks Saga will introduce Mastery cards, which will benefit you while you play. If you declare a Tokui-Waza, then Mastery cards stay in play at all times (just like a Main Personality) and cannot be affected or removed. In addition to looking cool, these cards boast new features. The unique design for Dragon Balls, Drills, and Mastery cards allow you to differentiate between these cards with just a glance. The Trunks Saga release will also feature new High Tech Level 2 cards, and a new foil design for commons, uncommons, rare and ultra-rare cards. The Trunks Saga release includes both Starter Decks complete with exclusive DBZ CCG Play Mats, and Booster Packs. The Trunks Saga release also features a revamped rulebook designed to make the game more manageable for players at all levels. "We regret that we've disappointed consumers by continuing to delay the release. We're anxious to get it in their hands! However, play testing and extensive market research have shown us that the changes being made to the Trunks Saga will significantly improve the game," said Chaz Elliott of Score.

I really have no comment about this, I've never gotten into CCG's. However, let's hope that Score's changes please the fans. Via the official site.

Mr.E @ 14:01 pst - Web Poll...
Now it's time to see what the fans think...would including the original japanese soundtrack on the uncut VHS tapes be enough of an incentive to buy them? I believe you all know my stance by now...VHS just ain't my thing. But, I know many of you still care about the format, so I'm conducting this poll to appease those who cling to their VCRs.

The old poll showed an overwhelming demand for android saga DVDs...looks like most of you do not care about chronological order (neither do I, hehe). FUNimation is going to be releasing half a dozen DVDs from all three sagas in the near future anyway, so everyone should be happy. ^_^

Mr.E @ 13:49 pst - When Will the Insanity End?
Even more shots of BFP, this time of their new Kami look-out map. I dunno, everything seems so shiney now. "I think I need to put some more glaze on the ham!" Still, the map itself is much improved. Have a look right here.

Update: More shots are available from the same map via the official site...
Monday - 01/29/2001

Mr.E @ 20:36 pst - New BFP Kame Hame Ha...
Small little BFP update today...a picture of the improved Kame Hame Ha graphic has been posted on their main page. Check it out...

Kaio @ 19:34 pst - More Fun with Theme Songs!
Thanks to Sechmet, we've obtained an MP3 of the German/Austrian Dragonball Z theme. If I do say so myself, it's an improvement on both the American and Canadian themes, however, I can't understand the lyrics, so it might be a bit different to one who actually knows what's being said.

Update: Whoops! Sechmet emailed me again, correcting me. This isn't a theme for Dragonball Z, since that in fact has not begun airing in Germany yet. It is a theme for Dragonball there. And thanks to Paul Kievits, who mailed us a translation. The lyrics are as follows:

Find all 7 dragonballs, and your wishes will come true.
Do you know the secret of the dragonballs?
There is magic power within them.
Son Goku, you are our hero.
You fight bravely across the world.
There are enemies everywhere
because everyone is looking for the dragonballs.
Find all 7 dragonballs, and your wishes will come true.
Do you know the secret of the dragonballs?
There is magic power within them.
Dragonballs...
 There you have it.

Sunday - 01/28/2001

Mr.E @ 21:50 pst - Bid For Power Goodies...
BFP has posted six sweet new shots on their main page. Damn, I love the new Big Bang look. Also, Planetquake has their BFP review up. The writer of this article seems to be more of a fan, and yet he still likes what he sees in the mod. Now to just get that new build on my machine... :)

Mr.E @ 21:41 pst - Interesting Anime Article...
This is circulating around the web community. The NY Times has published an article regarding anime and its "violence." I'm not going to argue that anime isn't violent, because it quite obviously is. We'd have to go into the whole cause & effect thing again. Here's the part of the article that interests us:

On "Dragon Ball Z," the Cartoon Network's most popular Japanese- style program, watched on average in more than a million households, Cell, an evil emperor, is approached by a cowering television news correspondent. He knocks the correspondent onto his back and then kicks him so hard that the man flies across a field head first into rocks, presumably dead.

What's your point? I don't think little Billy has the strength nor will to kick little Timmy like a football into a sharp pointy object dozens of yards away. Then again, a lot of American children are stupid. The point is made that Japan has one of the lowest violence rates in the world, but of course it is mentioned in fleeting. Headlines like "violent animated cartoons not causing violence, little children learn the meaning of friendship!" don't sell. Read the article here (You need to subscribe to view it. Don't worry, it's free). Thanks to the half a dozen people who submitted this! :P
Saturday - 01/27/2001

Mr.E @ 15:39 pst - More Techs, Etc...
Added half a dozen new attacks to the technique list. I applied the new format to the race list, and the bios now link to the big lists rather than pop-ups. Kid Buu, the last major character we needed from DBZ, has also been added. I'll be out until late Sunday...enjoy the updates!
Friday - 01/26/2001

Mr.E @ 23:32 pst - Content Update?
Settle down children, don't get your underpants in a bind. I've made a content update. A real one. Not just fan-submissions (but I do love those so). Our technique list has received a major overhaul...no more pop-ups, better images, more techs, several name corrections, and better descriptions. Our attack list has almost doubled in size, and I have about 10 more on-hand that I need to write (They will be posted very soon). The bios themselves still link to the pop-ups, but I'll eventually have them point to the big list. Those pop-ups were just too hard to maintain, too hard view, and causing too many errors. The race list will get a similar face-lift in a few weeks, then I will focus on writing more episode summaries (gah, falling behind again).

Special thanks to 007 and Napier who helped me with a couple of images/names!

Mr.E @ 17:29 pst - Quake3World.com's BFP Preview...
Pretty much what we said in our review, but he includes a few more juicy shots of BFP. He complains about Gohan and Kuririn's models, which I do know have been fixed since his build, and some of the maps being too plain. Here's his overall thought:

Overall, I really have to hand it to the Bid for Power team. They have a pretty good mod on their hands here, but the real test will be when the Dragon Ball Z fans get a hold of it.

I was quite surprised at how positive this review is considering the writer is not a DB fan in the least. That means this game has potential outside our little circle as well as within it. Check out the full preview here. Oh and, the Big Bang Attack and Masenko have been redone, and look better than ever. View them here and here respectively.

Kaio @ 16:35 pst - Censorship
I don't like to post things that aren't directly related to Dragonball, but this is really interesting, and does relate in a way. Check out this article by David Gauntlett. It argues that viewers are in fact unaffected by the media, and violent video games and television programs are not a factor in adolescent behavior. How much is really accomplished by censoring television shows? To put credit where it is due, I found the article via a link from PVP Online, a site that I frequent daily.

Mr.E @ 15:05 pst - The Second Coming...
Now now, before I'm attacked by every religious group out there, let me explain. Akira is going to be released in theatres! This is the film that got me into anime, and along with 2001, my favorite movie of all time. Here's the scoop from Yahoo:

HOLLYWOOD (Variety) - ``Akira,'' one of the earliest and most popular films in the Japanese anime film wave, is being restored in preparation for a theatrical re-release in the U.S. this spring.

Long Beach-based Pioneer Entertainment (USA), which specializes in Japanese animated programming, is spending $1 million on the restoration of the violent and gory science-fiction film released in 1988. Pioneer is planning to introduce the first DVD version of the cult favorite and a new VHS edition in the fourth quarter.

The picture, based on a comic book, follows teenage motorcycle gang members in post-World War III Tokyo who try to control one of their own members who rages out of control when he is given psychic powers as part of a military experiment.

The restoration being done by Intl. Video Conversions will include a high-definition mastering and a remixing of the audio components to create a soundtrack in Dolby Digital AC3 Surround Sound. A new English dub will also be created from a fresh translation of the original Japanese script.

One MEELION dollars? With that kind of cash to spend, the remastered edition is guaranteed to kick ass. And by theatrical release, do they mean widespread, or just a select few theaters (Hopefully they'll get as much circulation as Crouching Tiger)? Time will tell. This is a good day... :P

Web Poll:
Would you be more inclined to purchase the uncut DBZ tapes if they included the original Japanese music?

Yes, definitely!
No, the music does not affect my decision
No, I prefer the American music

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragonball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."

"Nope, the nose I'm using it for has only one nostril'"
-- Napier
